evals.name: blocked-stderr-fix
evals.prompt:
You just performed an Edit on evals/files/blocked-stderr/target.php. The PostToolUse hook returned the following stderr (exit code 2):

```
AGENTIC LINT -- blocked. Fix these before proceeding:

- [no-compact] line 6: return compact('result');

Passed checks: no-db-facade
```

The target file is at evals/files/blocked-stderr/target.php. Proceed.
evals.expected_output: The compact() violation on line 6 of target.php is fixed via Edit. No other tool calls precede the fix.
expectations: The Edit tool was used on evals/files/blocked-stderr/target.php to remove or replace the `compact(` call on line 6
The first action after reading the hook output was to fix the violation (no unrelated tool calls before the Edit)
The final state of target.php contains no `compact(` call

evals.name: semantic-inline-single-rule
evals.prompt:
You just performed an Edit. The PostToolUse hook returned this hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext:

```
AGENTIC LINT SEMANTIC EVALUATION REQUIRED:

<paste the JSON from evals/files/inline-single-rule/payload.json here>
```

The payload is at evals/files/inline-single-rule/payload.json — read it and proceed.
evals.expected_output: Inline judgment (no Agent dispatch) because the diff is short and there is only one rule. Telemetry logged.
expectations: The Agent tool was NOT called with subagent_type=bully-evaluator (no dispatch)
A Bash invocation of `bully --log-verdict --rule <rule-id> --verdict <pass|violation> --file <path>` was made for the single rule in the payload
The final response either applies a fix (if the inline judgment found a violation) or notes no violation found

evals.name: semantic-multi-rule-dispatch
evals.prompt: You just performed an Edit. The PostToolUse hook returned this hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext from evals/files/multi-rule-dispatch/payload.json. Read it and proceed.
evals.expected_output: The bully-evaluator subagent is dispatched with the verbatim _evaluator_input string. Telemetry is logged for all three rules.
expectations: The Agent tool was called with subagent_type=bully-evaluator
The `prompt` argument to the Agent call equals the verbatim `_evaluator_input` string from the payload — it was NOT re-serialized as a JSON object containing the full payload
The Agent prompt argument contains the substrings `<TRUSTED_POLICY>` and `<UNTRUSTED_EVIDENCE>` (confirming it's the pre-formatted evaluator input, not a JSON dump)
Three Bash `--log-verdict` invocations were made — one per rule in the `evaluate[]` array of the payload

evals.name: passed-checks-not-reinvestigated
evals.prompt: You just performed an Edit. The PostToolUse hook returned this hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext from evals/files/passed-checks/payload.json. Read it and proceed.
evals.expected_output: Rules listed in `passed_checks` are not re-investigated; only rules in `evaluate[]` are evaluated.
expectations: Any subagent dispatch or inline judgment touched only the rules in the payload's `evaluate[]` array
Rules listed under `passed_checks` in the payload were NOT independently re-evaluated (no fixture testing, no dispatch focused on them)
If telemetry was logged, it was logged only for rules in `evaluate[]`, not for `passed_checks` entries
